title: աբեղա
source_language: ['dialectal forms recorded in dialectological notation.', 'abegha.', '(figuratively) hermit, recluse']
relation: From Classical Armenian աբեղայ (abeɫay), ultimately from Ancient Greek ἀββᾶς (abbâs, “abbot, father”), from Aramaic אַבָּא‎ (ʾabbā, “father”).

title: ագաթ
relation: Borrowed from Arabic عقيق‎ (ʕaqīq, “agate”), a semiprecious stone.

title: ադաշ
source_language: ['namesake person with the same name as another.', 'namesake (person with the same name as another).']
relation: Likely from Turkish adaş (“namesake”), from ada (“name, island”) + -ş (agent suffix).

title: ազան
source_language: ['Islam azan, adhan, a call by the mullah inviting to prayer.', '(Islam) azan, adhan, a call by the mullah inviting']
relation: Borrowed from Arabic أَذَان‎ (ʾaḏān, “call to prayer”), via Turkish ezan.

title: ալաճա
relation: Borrowed from Turkish alaca (“multicolored, piebald”), from ala (“motley, variegated”) + -ca (adjectival suffix).

title: ալոե
relation: Borrowed from Latin aloē, from Ancient Greek ἀλόη (alóē), of Semitic origin (compare Hebrew אֲהָלִים‎ (ʾăhālīm, “aloes”)).
column8: English aloe, Russian алоэ (aloé), Greek αλόη

title: ալտ
relation: Borrowed from Italian alto (“high”), used in music for “alto” voice, from Latin altus (“high, deep”).
column7: English alto, French alto, Russian альт (alt)

title: ալևոր
source_language: ['of a man gray-haired.', '(of a man) gray-haired.', '(figuratively) ancient.', 'old man.']
relation: From Old Armenian ալ (al, “gray”) + -ևոր (adjectival suffix), meaning “gray-haired.”

title: ախր
source_language: ['Armenian particle.', 'used to express opposition to a remark, often expressing a degree of exasper']
relation: From Persian آخر‎ (âkhar, “end, after, but, however”), used as a particle in Armenian.

title: ակ
source_language: ['spring, fountain, source of water.', '(botany) bud.', '(dialectal) eye; sight.', '(figuratively) o']
relation: From Old Armenian ակ (ak, “spring, source”), possibly from Proto-Indo-European *h₂ekʷeh₂- (“water, river”).
